content:
Key Findings

  • Hyndburn Borough Council's total analysed spending over ten financial years, from 2016/17 to 2025/26, amounted to £211.3M.
  • The Resources & Finance department accounted for the largest share of spending, totalling £119.0M, which represents 56.3% of the council's expenditure.
  • Environmental Services was the second highest spending area, with an outlay of £34.8M, making up 16.5% of the total.
  • Regeneration & Housing received £31.5M, comprising 14.9% of the council's spending across the analysed period.
  • The smallest departmental spends included Planning & Transportation at £2.9M (1.4%) and Environmental Health at £2.2M (1.0%).

Hyndburn's Financial Landscape: A Decade of Spending

Hyndburn Borough Council's financial activities provide a clear picture of its operational priorities and service delivery. Over a ten-year period, spanning from the 2016/17 financial year through to 2025/26, the council's total expenditure reached a substantial £211.3M. This comprehensive dataset, published under the Local Government Transparency Code, offers valuable insights into how public funds are allocated across various departments and services within the district.

Understanding these spending patterns is crucial for residents and stakeholders alike. The figures reveal not only the scale of the council's financial responsibilities but also the relative importance of different service areas. While the total sum of £211.3M represents the aggregate spending, a deeper dive into departmental allocations highlights the core functions driving the council's day-to-day operations and long-term strategic goals.

Dominant Departments: Resources and Core Services

An analysis of Hyndburn Borough Council's spending reveals a significant concentration of funds within specific departments. The Resources & Finance department stands out as the primary recipient of council funds, accounting for an impressive £119.0M. This figure represents 56.3% of the total expenditure, indicating that financial management, internal services, and corporate functions form the bedrock of the council's operations.

Following Resources & Finance, two other key departments absorb a substantial portion of the council's budget. Environmental Services allocated £34.8M, making up 16.5% of the total spend. This department typically covers essential services such as waste collection, street cleaning, and park maintenance, directly impacting residents' quality of life. The Regeneration & Housing department also saw significant investment, with £31.5M, or 14.9% of the total, directed towards initiatives aimed at improving local areas and addressing housing needs.

Rank Department Amount % of Total
1 Resources & Finance £119.0M 56.3%
2 Environmental Services £34.8M 16.5%
3 Regeneration & Housing £31.5M 14.9%
4 Other £17.3M 8.2%
5 Legal & Democratic £3.6M 1.7%
6 Planning & Transportation £2.9M 1.4%
7 Environmental Health £2.2M 1.0%

Supporting Functions and Smaller Portfolios

Beyond the top three spending areas, other departments play vital, albeit smaller, roles in the council's overall expenditure. The category labelled Other accounts for £17.3M, representing 8.2% of the total spend. This category often encompasses various cross-cutting costs or smaller, less frequently categorised expenditures that support the broader council functions.

Essential governance and regulatory functions are managed by departments with more modest budgets. Legal & Democratic services received £3.6M, or 1.7% of the total, ensuring the council operates within legal frameworks and facilitates democratic processes. Planning & Transportation, crucial for local development and infrastructure, accounted for £2.9M, which is 1.4% of the total. Finally, Environmental Health, responsible for public health and safety regulations, had an expenditure of £2.2M, making up 1.0% of the council's total analysed spending.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Hyndburn Borough Council about the specific breakdown of spending within the Resources & Finance department.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to hear discussions about departmental budgets and service delivery.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see details of major agreements with suppliers across all departments.
  • Write to your local councillor about the allocation of funds to Environmental Services or Regeneration & Housing in your specific area.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data for Hyndburn Borough Council and other Lancashire councils yourself.
This article is based on Hyndburn Borough Council's published spending data covering 2016/17 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
